purpose: Validate the owner-reported PyGRC reset-baseline persistence correction and its restoration-identity consequences before re-admitting the updated graph revision.
evidence_effect: Generic source/import/reset/provider conformance only; no realization, dependence mode, candidate behavior, restoration claim beyond the tested contract, or scientific evidence.

rcae_entry_authority.identity_rule: Committed RCAE HEAD plus exact SHA-256 of each pre-validation worktree artifact; separate from both graph revisions.

graph_revision_transition.required_worktree_state: clean before and after every inspection, import, or dynamic check
graph_revision_transition.portable_identity_rule: Repository ID plus full revision plus repository-relative path plus SHA-256; local checkout path is raw provenance only.

changed_scope_discovery.source: git diff --name-status and exact file diffs between previous_admitted_revision and proposed_revision
included: Every changed path between the two exact revisions
Every prior admitted source whose bytes or public contract changed
Every new source, test, contract, documentation, closeout, or public export used to support the reset correction
LGRC9V3 construction, reset, state replacement, snapshot, save, load, restoration identity, and digest callables
Any shared GRC9V3 or snapshot machinery changed by the correction
changed_scope_discovery.new_path_rule: Record the path and role under P2-I2-CHG-006 before treating it as authority.
changed_scope_discovery.new_callable_rule: Record its public identity, source, signature, accepted/unsupported shape, causal relevance, and claim boundary before admission.

validation_questions: What exact serialized artifact owns the reset baseline, and is it non-recursive and complete?
Does native save/load preserve both current restoration identity and reset outcome?
Can two models with equal current state but different reset baselines still share restoration identity?
Do reset-baseline-only differences change the identity document and digest?
Do repeated save/load cycles preserve the same baseline and reset outcome?
Does set_state preserve or rebase the reset baseline, and is any rebase operation explicit?
What provider artifact schema/version now governs the result?
What happens for legacy snapshots without an explicit reset-baseline artifact?
Do malformed, partial, wrong-family, or unsupported-version baseline payloads fail explicitly without fallback?
Are imports and every affected callable tied to the proposed checkout?
Does the graph worktree remain clean and unchanged?
Does the correction leave provider and realization selection to I03/I06?

allowed: Force imports from the proposed checkout using PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 and PYTHONPATH=${GRC}/src.
Construct minimal generic LGRC9V3 states with no P2-I2 source set, pool, cell, control, comparator, or response.
Mutate only generic current-state and reset-baseline markers needed to distinguish identity and reset behavior.
Run focused upstream reset/restoration tests with pytest cache disabled and temporary files outside the graph repository.
Independently recompute canonical identity digests and exact reset-state equality.
prohibited: Dependency installation, download, editable install, ambient package substitution, or graph-repository write.
Any P2-I2 combined-versus-single, label, access, carrier, pool, cell, control, calibration, candidate, or response behavior.
Fallback after provider or compatibility error.
Use of generic conformance output as realization adequacy or scientific evidence.

required_outputs.source_transition: Machine-readable exact old/new changed-source and contract record
required_outputs.report: Narrative I02R2 revalidation and gate disposition
required_outputs.admission_update: Updated exact manifest or retained failed re-admission record

failure_effects.source_or_import_failure: Do not admit the proposed revision; retain prior admission as historical only and keep I03 blocked.
failure_effects.reset_persistence_failure: Retain reset as unsupported_blocks_branching; do not claim the upstream gap closed; keep I03 blocked pending explicit disposition.
failure_effects.identity_coverage_failure: Block reset-dependent branch comparison and fail source admission for the proposed provider contract.
failure_effects.compatibility_ambiguity: Do not infer a legacy policy; retain the exact ambiguity and keep the gate open or failed.
failure_effects.graph_mutation: Stop, retain the dirty-state failure, and assign no scientific effect.
failure_effects.i03_leakage: Remove or quarantine the leaked choice and reopen every affected later gate.

exit_rule: All exact source/import/reset/save/load/identity/schema/compatibility checks pass and the graph remains clean, or the precise failed condition is retained with I03 blocked.
